rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a severe internal contradiction: the reported numbers show self-preference falling from 62.1% to 49.3% after fine-tuning, yet the stated interpretation asserts 'improved self-recognition yields stronger self-preference.' The later cross-model regression claim ('each percentage-point increase in recognition translates into a rise in preference') repeats this contradiction. Even a prose-only cleanup cannot responsibly leave this coherence failure intact without flagging it.
rationale.R1.3: The regression slope (β = 0.41, positive direction) and the cross-model narrative are inconsistent with the primary Llama-3-70B evidence, where the fine-tuning intervention produced the opposite directional effect. Evidence-claim alignment is therefore materially at stake regardless of whether the delegation explicitly requested it.
rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' maps load-bearingly onto writing polish: sentence concision, redundancy removal (e.g., 'direct causal evidence' restated across two consecutive sentences), hedge calibration, and paragraph-level flow. Failure to improve prose quality would invalidate the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I limit changes to prose-level polish (concision, flow, redundancy) only, or should I also identify and correct the logical inconsistency where the reported data (self-preference dropping from 62.1% to 49.3% post-fine-tuning) directly contradicts the stated causal interpretation that improved self-recognition produces stronger self-preference?
